headline: True Crime Juggernaut 'World's Most Evil Killers' Hits Milestone 200-Episode Run
lede: Radial Entertainment has secured the entire 10-season catalog of its breakout true crime franchise, signaling a robust market for serialized documentary programming.
body:
The gritty documentary series "World's Most Evil Killers" has carved an impressive niche in the true crime landscape, reaching a landmark 200-episode milestone that underscores the genre's insatiable audience appetite.

Radial Entertainment's strategic acquisition locks down all ten seasons of the provocative series, with Seasons 9 and 10 primed to debut on the company's FilmRise channel this month. Each season comprises 20 hour-long episodes, collectively representing more than 150 hours of meticulously researched criminal narratives.

The series has distinguished itself through granular investigations into the psychology and methodologies of notorious criminals, moving beyond sensationalism to deliver forensic storytelling. Its sustained success reflects a broader industry trend of specialized true crime content finding robust viewership across multiple platforms.

Industry analysts note that such long-running documentary series represent significant value propositions for streaming and cable networks seeking cost-effective, compelling programming. The "World's Most Evil Killers" franchise demonstrates how deep-dive documentary formats can generate sustained audience engagement without the high production costs associated with scripted series.
key_facts: More than 150 hours of total programming
